title: quercus faginea var. subocculata
intro: Quercus faginea var. subocculata, also known as the Corsican oak or dwarf evergreen oak, is a rare and endemic evergreen tree native to the island of Corsica. The medicinal properties of Quercus faginea var. subocculata lie in its leaves, which contain tannins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of infections. Quercus faginea var. subocculata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce stress and anxiety.
